evals.prompt:
running /next on main. TaskList returns 3 tasks for this project:
- Task #10: priority P1, status pending, blockedBy: [11]
- Task #11: priority P2, status pending, no blockers
- Task #12: priority P2, status in_progress, no blockers, has metadata.design populated

what do you pick?
evals.expected_output: Skips Task #10 (blocked by #11 which isn't completed). At P2, prefers Task #12 (in_progress) over #11 (pending). Reads #12 via TaskGet, classifies based on signals (has design + no children -> /develop). Presents recommendation via AskUserQuestion.
expectations: Recognizes Task #10 is blocked by #11 (not completed) and skips it despite highest priority
At same priority (P2), prefers Task #12 (in_progress) over #11 (pending)
Shows what it picked and why via AskUserQuestion before dispatching

evals.prompt:
running /next on branch alice/add-auth. gh pr view shows PR #15 (draft, CI failing). gh pr checks shows 2 failures: lint and unit tests. there are 2 unresolved review comments too.

what do you suggest?
evals.expected_output: Gathers full PR state (review, CI, comments). Suggests fixing CI as top priority since CI failing outranks addressing comments. Presents via AskUserQuestion with alternatives.
expectations: Gathers PR state including review decision, CI status, and comments
Suggests fixing CI failures as highest-priority action — CI failing takes precedence over unresolved comments
Presents via AskUserQuestion with alternatives, does not auto-start fixing

evals.prompt:
running /next on main. TaskList returns:
- Task #20: priority P2, status pending, title 'Brainstorm: notification system', metadata.design populated, status_detail 'review'
- Task #21: priority P3, status pending, title 'Add retry logic to webhook sender', type 'bug'
- Task #22: priority P3, status in_progress, title 'Refactor config loader', no design

what do you pick?
evals.expected_output: Skips Task #20 because status_detail is 'review'. Between #21 and #22 at P3, prefers #22 (in_progress). Reads #22 — no design means route to /scope. Presents recommendation.
expectations: Skips Task #20 — status_detail 'review' is excluded from candidate pool
Picks Task #22 (in_progress preferred over #21 pending at same priority)
Routes #22 to /scope since it has no design — shows rationale before dispatching

evals.prompt:
running /next --auto on main. TaskList returns one task:
- Task #30: priority P1, status pending, type 'bug', title 'Login page crashes on empty email'

what do you do?
evals.expected_output: Picks Task #30 (only candidate, P1, unblocked). Reads it, classifies as bug -> /debugging. Because --auto is set, invokes /debugging directly without presenting alternatives via AskUserQuestion.
expectations: Picks Task #30 as the only unblocked candidate
